betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Capacitors§f

§aPower storage workhorses — from 600 kHE to 6 GHE of rapid-discharge capacity!§f

§bCapacitors§f are power storage devices capable of discharging large amounts of power rapidly, making them vital for power banks supporting high-power operations.

§6Tiers§f:

§b• Copper Capacitor§f — 600 kHE, 1 kHE/t (Chemical Plant)
§b• Gold Capacitor§f — 6 MHE, 10 kHE/t (Assembly Machine)
§b• Niobium Capacitor§f — 60 MHE, 100 kHE/t (Assembly Factory)
§b• Tantalum Capacitor§f — 300 MHE, 500 kHE/t (Assembly Factory)
§b• Bismuth Capacitor§f — 1.5 GHE, 2.5 MHE/t (Assembly Factory + QPU)
§b• Spark Capacitor§f — 6 GHE, 10 MHE/t (Assembly Factory + Cold Perfluoromethyl)

§eTip§f: Use copper capacitors for early power buffers, tantalum for mid-game systems, and Spark Capacitors for end-game setups like the Particle Accelerator and Inertial Confinement Fusion Reactor. Higher tier capacitors charge and discharge faster — vital for burst-power machines!
